sections.title: Starting Out
Meeting the wanderer

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, wanderer.

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Hello to you too, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Hiss!

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Aghhh get that cat away from me. Quickly please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why? What's your problem with it?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Just take it away please. Take it away. If you do, I'll tell you of the secret passageway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What secret passage?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Get the cat away from me first and then I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, now where is this passage?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: It's by that group of rocks just to the north east, but you won't be able to enter. Well, not yet anyway...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where does it lead to?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: To Sophanem, the city of the dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sophanem?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: The second city of the Menaphites, full of giant monuments to their departed.

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: The place is locked down at the moment, so the passage is the only way in. Apparently, they've been hit by a set of plagues. That pathetic new High Priest of Icthlarin is probably to blame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... Tell me about this Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Well fair enough then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why do you say I won't be able to enter the passage?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: First bring me some supplies and then I'll tell you. I'm running low and I'm about to embark on a long journey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Get me a full waterskin and a tinderbox. I seem to have lost my own and these desert nights are very cold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I actually have those on me already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get you your supplies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I've got other things to do.

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Fool! Only I can give you the knowledge on the secret passageway!

sections.title: Talking to the Wanderer, or using a full waterskin or tinderbox on her

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Hiss

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Get that cat away from me!

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: You again. Do you have my supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I have them here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you need again?

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Bring me a full waterskin and a tinderbox.

sections.title: Entering the Wanderer's tent

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yuck! It smells like there's dead cats in there or something. I'm not going in.

sections.title: When the player has all the supplies

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Good... Now, look into my eyes...

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Look deeply into them...

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: Don't blink...

sections.title: City of the Dead
Talking to Klenter

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Spawn of evil!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Organ snatcher!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Thief!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Evil doer!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Grave defiler!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: You pathetic excuse for a thief!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: You worm! May the Devourer take your soul!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Grave robber!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Wooo wooo wooooo!

sections.title: Talking to Jex
Attempting to climb down the nearby ladder

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Hey! You don't have permission to go down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: I will not speak with a defiler of the dead. Be gone!

sections.title: Talking to a Worker
Talking to Raetul

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: Get away! You're a thief and a desecrator of the dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: You heard me! Clear off!

sections.title: Talking to Siamun

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Clear off! I'll have nothing to do with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Go!

sections.title: Talking to the Carpenter

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Get away from me you filthy grave robber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... okay.

sections.title: Talking to the Embalmer

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Clear off you evil body snatcher! You won't get any of my wares.

sections.title: Talking to a Priest
Talking to a priest in the temple

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: What business do you have with a servant of Icthlarin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help figuring out what's going on.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: I will not aid a defiler of the dead. Be gone!

sections.title: Talking to a priest by a gate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Can I go through this gate?

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: No. Nobody may enter or leave Sophanem for any reason due to the quarantine. Move along.

sections.title: Attempting to open a nearby gate

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Sorry, but Sophanem is under quarantine. No one may leave or enter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm not infected with anything!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: I'm afraid my orders are clear.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You! I will have no dealings with those who plunder the houses of the dead.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Get thee gone from my presence!

sections.title: Talking to the Sphinx
Talking to the Sphinx again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me. I need help.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: We've already had this conversation, have we not? You're in a spot of bother, but you can't remember what happened to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty much.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Perhaps you should try to find something to remind you again. I find that important objects and locations can trigger memories, no matter how repressed they are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah, how interesting... a cat. Come here to me, kitty.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me. I need help.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Yes, your companion tells me that you are in a spot of bother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What companion? Oh, you mean my pet cat?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea what happened to me? One moment I was talking to a wanderer in the desert, the next I was here. I also have a cracking headache.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: A wanderer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. She had red hair, and weird red eyes as well.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Interesting, very interesting. I'm afraid I do not know what happened to you, but it sounds like quite a bit occurred between you meeting that wanderer and now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I can't remember any of it.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Then I suggest you find something to remind you. I find that important objects and locations can trigger memories, no matter how repressed they are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, my poor head. What's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did I get here? That wanderer... This is her doing!

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: You foul thief, return what is mine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: You heard me! Restore what you have stolen from my tomb!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Klenter
lines.text: Wooo wooo wooooo!

sections.title: Figuring out what on Gielinor is going on
Flashback 1: Touching the door of Klenter's pyramid
Attempting to climb up the ladder during the flashback

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must get to the heard of the pyramid...

sections.title: Solving the puzzle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I remember! That wanderer, she hypnotised me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... I can't remember why. I needed to get... something. This is so confusing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe someone around here can tell me what on Gielinor is going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must go to the heart of the pyramid... The mistress commands it...

sections.title: Trying to enter the pyramid again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drat! It's locked.

sections.title: Asking the Sphinx for help

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I have no interest in speaking with you. You should bring your cat to me again. The two of us have much to discuss.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah, how interesting... a cat. Come here to me, kitty.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to regain some of my memories.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Yes, your cat tells me you're in a spot of bother. Something to do with grave robbing, tisk tisk. So unbecoming of a guardian of cats.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Yes, your cat told me already. Something to do with grave robbing, tisk tisk. So unbecoming of a guardian of cats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me? Please?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Well, I have to say I enjoy a laugh at someone's expense now and again... so let's have a bit of fun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't like the sound of this.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I'll help you if you can solve a simple puzzle of mine, but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's always a 'but', isn't there?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: ... but if you answer incorrectly, I get to keep your cat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, that sounds fair.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: A husband and wife have six sons and each son has one sister. How many people are in the family?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Totally positive.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I think your master has proven that [he/she] is too foolish to look after you. Come with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, come on! You can't take my cat. I've spent so long training and looking after it!

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Well, you should have thought of that before you risked it for some advice from a crazy old Sphinx.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you at least help me now?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: What was it you needed help with? Oh yes, I remember now. You're a grave robber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know. I don't want to risk my cat.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: How disappointing. Still, nothing ventured, nothing gained. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Totally positive.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Well answered, human. I guess you get to keep your cat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, will you help me?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: With your grave robbing problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: How disappointing. Still, nothing ventured, nothing gained. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want to risk my cat.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: How disappointing. Still, nothing ventured, nothing gained. Goodbye.

sections.title: Being accused of robbing a grave after answering the Sphinx's riddle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't rob anybody's grave!

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Too true, you didn't rob just anybody's grave. You managed to rob the grave of Klenter, the recently deceased High Priest of Icthlarin. Do you know what that means?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... no.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Icthlarin is the Menaphite god of the dead. By desecrating the tomb of one of his priests, you've made an enemy out of every person in this city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I didn't desecrate any tomb! There's no proof!

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Really? Because if I'm not mistaken, that's Klenter's shade right over there. He definitely doesn't seem happy with you. There's also the burial jar in your possession. How do you explain that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know about the burial jar?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I can smell the rotting organs from here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I don't know exactly what happened. All I know is that wanderer hypnotised me and had me steal something from this pyramid.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: The burial jar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I guess so. It's all still very unclear.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Interesting. I'm afraid I can't help you. I have my own concerns without involving myself with petty human affairs.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: However, I suggest you seek out the new High Priest of Icthlarin to the south east. He might be able to assist you. Of course, you'll need to convince him to talk to you first.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Here, I have a token that he will know is from me. Take that to him, and I'm sure he will speak with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go speak with the High Priest.

sections.title: Talking to the Sphinx after receiving the Sphinx's token

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I have no interest in speaking with you. You should bring your cat to me again. The two of us have much to discuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you stole my last one!

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Well, you would be well advised to get another.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah, how interesting... a cat. Come here to me, kitty.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that token you gave me. Could I have another please?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah, you humans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I am an agent of neither good nor evil. I am unconcerned with the plight of mankind and their petty wars and beliefs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, now that you've told me what you're not, can you perhaps tell me what you are?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I am the guardian of feline kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would they need a guardian?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Cats play a significant role in the religion of this area, particularly when it comes to guarding the dead from the Devourer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Devourer?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: She's the very incarnation of destruction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: What understanding do you have of the Menaphite gods?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Little to none.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Well the Menaphite Pantheon is made up of four main deities.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: The two main gods of the pantheon are Tumeken, the god of light, and Elidinis, the goddess of fertility.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Tumeken and Elidinis also have two children. Icthlarin is one, and the Devourer is the other.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They named her the Devourer? Not great at parenting, were they?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: She was not always called such. She is known by her deeds now, rather than her name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if the Devourer is the bad sibling, does that make Icthlarin the good one?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: That is the common belief, but things are rarely that simple. In some ways, neither of them are good or bad.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Icthlarin is the god of the dead. He takes care of the passing of souls to their next life. The Devourer is the goddess of destruction, and that is what she craves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you said she wasn't evil?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: That's not quite what I said. Still, is destruction enough to make one evil? Fire destroys, but is that evil?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... no, but it also creates heat.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: We could have an overlong theological debate now, but let's instead get to my point. Destruction has a place, so maybe the Devourer does as well. However, she has taken it too far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: The Devourer wishes to destroy every soul in existence, living or dead. As you can imagine, this has brought her into conflict with Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see where this is going. The cats are used to protect the dead from the Devourer, like you said earlier. But who protects the cats?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Well anticipated. The Devourer is terrified of cats for reasons I will not explain. However, her followers, although few in number, are not. I ensure the survival of cats. For this service, Icthlarin grants me eternal life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, then, you are a follower of Icthlarin?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Nothing of the sort. As I've said before, I have no interest in humans and their gods. I look after my kind. For that, Icthlarin looks after me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is all very interesting, but I've got to get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on in this city?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I thought that would be pretty obvious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I could benefit from your point of view. You seem the independent type.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Flattery, my adventurer, will get you everywhere. But to answer your question, Sophanem has been struck with plagues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More than one?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Quite a number. There's a plague of frogs to the west. In the east, you can also find locusts.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: The cows have only been producing sour milk since the outbreak, and the residents have been struck down by a rash of spots.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The residents? There are a lot of living people here for a city of the dead.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Sophanem is called the city of the dead because the dead are brought here. Death plays a rather large role in the beliefs of the people and, as a result, the business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is all very interesting, but I've got to get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go speak with the High Priest.

sections.title: Talking to a priest after receiving the Sphinx's token

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: What do you want, cursed one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Sphinx told me to find the High Priest of Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: A likely story. She would never reduce herself to talk to one as lowly as you, never mind help you!

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest after receiving the Sphinx's token

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You! I will have no dealings with those who plunder the houses of the dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! The Sphinx told me that I should talk to you.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The Sphinx? Is it possible? That contrary old cat has never done anything to help us. Prove it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She gave me a token to show you, but I lost it.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well then stop wasting my time! Can't you see I'm trying to put a stop to these plagues?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well this is... unexpected...

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: So what do you want from me, thief?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I appreciate it doesn't look great right now, but I'm not actually a bad person.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well you could have fooled me! Good people don't rob from the dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't me! Well... it was, I guess. I didn't do it on purpose though. I was hypnotised!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hypnotised?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! There was this wanderer! She had me break into that pyramid to steal... a burial jar... I think. It's all still a bit confusing.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: This is all a disaster! The plagues were bad enough, but now we've got hypnotised adventurers plundering tombs! I've only just started this job as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do seem to be going through a rough patch. Do you know what caused the plagues?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We fear they are a punishment sent by the gods. Perhaps we have displeased Icthlarin in some way. Maybe I am to blame. All this only started after I became High Priest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not sure if I can help with your plagues, but I need to fix whatever mess I caused in that pyramid. Can you help me?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Oh, er... I suppose the burial jar you took must be returned as soon as possible. That will allow my predecessor to rest once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can do that. Do you have any advice for me when it comes to the pyramid?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I don't think so... No wait! There is something important.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: As with many of our tombs, the pyramid has magical seals to protect it from the Devourer, the goddess of destruction. The fact that you were able to get in before means that you must have a cat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... yes. But how did you know that?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Cats act as guardians against the Devourer. I'm surprised the Sphinx didn't tell you this. As such, only a cat can open the pyramid door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I guess that makes sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Putting Things Right
Talking to the High Priest after being told to return the jar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: One of my priests found this lying outside the pyramid. Please try to be more respectful of our dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops. I'll make sure I look after it this time. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Have you managed to return the jar yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Please hurry. The people are beginning to... well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Question your sanity?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: No, just my authority. Don't forget that to enter the pyramid, you'll need a cat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is it so important that the jar be returned?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well those jars are a key part of our burial practices. To remove one is to directly insult not just Icthlarin, but all those that have passed on to the next life.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Not to mention, it wasn't just any jar you took. You stole a jar from the tomb of our former High Priest! Why do you think his ghost is now wandering the city once more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what will happen if I don't return the jar?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Have you not noticed the plagues already filling our city? How long before Klenter calls upon Icthlarin to send a few more?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: For all we know, the plagues we already have could be punishment for your grave robbing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, don't go blaming me for that. Those plagues were here before I even met that wanderer!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Perhaps Icthlarin foresaw what would happen and sent the plagues as a pre-emptive punishment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really think he'd do that?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I don't know... Icthlarin is a patient and forgiving god. It does not make sense to me that he would do this to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he's not the one responsible.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Perhaps. Either way, that jar needs to be returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about the plagues.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: They started a few days ago. We have locusts in the east of the city and frogs in the west. The cows have started to produce sour milk, and all of us have become infected with some sort of pox.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Once it became clear how bad it was, Menaphos, our neighbouring city, closed their gates to us and everyone else. We closed our own gates soon after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That can't have been an easy choice to make.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I didn't want to, but the other priests insisted. We had to stop the plagues from spreading. It seems that our choice was the right one. So far, the plagues have remained contained, but at a great cost.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Many of our citizens are trapped outside the city. Some are stuck in Menaphos, with more in other parts of the desert.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: However, that may well be for the best. Without trade from other settlements, we've been struggling to even feed the few of us left in the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much longer do you think you can last?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Not long. We need to find a way to stop the plagues soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Icthlarin is the son of Tumeken and Elidinis and brother to the Devourer. He is the Menaphite god of the dead. He is responsible for ensuring the safe passage of all souls from this life to the next.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Here in Sophanem, we support Icthlarin in his role by making sure the dead are suitably prepared for their journey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is Icthlarin only responsible for those that die in the desert?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: No. While Icthlarin is only worshipped here, his responsibilities stretch across all of Gielinor. He cares not for an individual's beliefs. All who pass on, enter his care.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I imagine there are plenty of people out there who have a different take on things.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Which they are welcome to. It does not change the facts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem very sure in these 'facts'.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: While most of the gods, Tumeken and Elidinis included, may be long gone from Gielinor, Icthlarin remains. His important role in caring for the dead means that he is still able to interact with us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've met him?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well... no. While he is able to walk among us if he desires, he chooses not to so as to respect our way of life. Not to mention, his role requires his constant attention.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about the Devourer.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Long ago, back when Tumeken and Elidinis still walked these lands, they had two children. Icthlarin, god of the dead, was one. The other was Amascut, the goddess of rebirth.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: For a time, Icthlarin and Amascut worked together in harmony. Icthlarin would ensure most souls are safely guided to the next life, while Amascut would select a small number to be reborn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But something happened?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Yes. During a great war over these lands, Amascut became corrupted. She was warped into the Devourer, becoming the goddess of destruction.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Since then, her only goal has been to devour every soul in existence, both living and dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Sphinx told me something similar, but her interpretation was a bit different.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We here have a lot of respect for the Sphinx. However, you should be careful of her words. She does not know the gods like we priests do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is the Devourer still around?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Unfortunately so. Most gods have long since left this world. However, the Devourer was able to find a way to remain.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Luckily for us, some of her former priestesses were able to weaken her, but she still poses a great threat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Talking to a priest in the temple after being told to return the jar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Cursed one, why have you not yet returned the burial jar of our old leader?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on that still.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: I wonder how someone as insignificant as you even managed to get inside the pyramid, never mind reach its heart.

sections.title: Talking to Jex after being told to return the jar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Hello there. Welcome to the Temple of the Lesser Gods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Talking to the Sphinx after being told to return the jar

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I have no interest in speaking with you. You should bring your cat to me again. The two of us have much to discuss.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah, how interesting... a cat. Come here to me, kitty.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meow.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah, human. How go things, great guardian of cats?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you mocking me?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I don't need to mock you, human. You're doing a good enough job of that on your own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. Come on, cat. Lets leave the nasty Sphinx alone.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meow!

sections.title: Entering Klenter's pyramid
Entering the pyramid again after leaving

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think the High Priest mentioned something about needing a cat to enter the pyramid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey puss! Think you can open this door again?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meeeow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think the High Priest mentioned something about needing a cat to enter the pyramid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey puss! What do you reckon about that cat on the door? Do you think you can open it?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meeeow.

sections.title: Flashback 2: Jumping over the pit
Attempting to take the wrong jar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want this jar.

sections.title: Attempting to take the correct jar

lines.speaker: Apparition
lines.text: You will not defile this place!

sections.title: Taking the jar after defeating the apparition
Leaving through the western hallway

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I really did steal the jar. I need to return it as fast as I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get the burial jar for the mistress...

sections.title: Returning the jar
Attempting to take a jar again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already caused enough trouble over these jars. I'll just leave them alone.

sections.title: Talking to a priest after returning the jar

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Bother me not. I am preparing for the ceremony. I'm sure even you can curb your trivial demands until after that.

sections.title: Informing the High Priest of the jar's return

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Have you managed to return the jar yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's done.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Wonderful! Now we can prepare for the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... ceremony?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The ceremony to reconsecrate Klenter's tomb of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would have been nice for you to mention this earlier.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Sorry, with all this plague business, it must have slipped my mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're the High Priest of Icthlarin, but you forgot a key ceremony?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well if you want to try running a city with multiple plagues, you're welcome to take over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not what I... Never mind.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Good. Now, this ceremony is very dangerous, so we must ensure everything is done correctly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dangerous? How?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well the pyramid has these magical seals on it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... to protect it from the Devourer. Yes, you told me earlier.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Right! Of course I did. Well for us to properly reconsecrate the tomb, we must first remove those seals. That means that once we begin, the pyramid will be vulnerable until the ceremony is complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Now, we made many of the preparations while you were gone, but the carpenter and the embalmer could both use a hand. Could you go and speak with them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I have to help?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Did you forget that this is all your fault?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I assumed that by returning the jar I stole, my job would be done.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The job will be done when Klenter is at rest once more.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Besides, thanks to the plagues, we're not allowing people to leave the city. However, as you seem to be unaffected, I've told the guards to make an exception for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that means I'm best placed to help. Fine. The carpenter and the embalmer, did you say?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: That's right. We need the carpenter to create a new holy symbol for the ceremony. He lives over to the east. The embalmer also needs help gathering some supplies. He lives just south of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Ceremony Preparations
Talking to the High Priest after being asked to help with the ceremony

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Have you helped the embalmer and the carpenter yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The embalmer lives just south of here and the carpenter lives over to the east. Please hurry. We need to get this ceremony performed before some other bad thing happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Talking to a priest by a gate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Can I go through this gate?

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Yes. The High Priest has given you permission to come and go as you please.

sections.title: Aiding the Embalmer
Collecting tree sap
Using the suntrap
Buying linen from Raetul

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: Good afternoon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it?

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: It's hot and sunny, which is good enough for me. So can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met with the town's embalmer and he mentioned that he needed a few things, linen being one of them.

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: Well I can sell you a sheet of linen for 30 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much on me. I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: Of course. That will be 30 coins please and thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Raetul
lines.text: Do come again.

sections.title: Talking to the Embalmer again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Oh, it's you. Any luck obtaining those items?

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Thank you. I think that's everything I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. I guess I should go and see how the carpenter is getting on.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Well you'd better get a move on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get tree sap?

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: You can use a knife to extract some from a tree, but make sure you have a bucket ready. Most coniferous evergreen trees should have some. They're the cone- shaped ones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get salt?

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Normally I get mine from the salt lake just north of the city. Just fill a bucket from the lake and then use the suntrap outside to separate the salt from the water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get linen?

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: The local cloth salesman should have some for sale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get going.

sections.title: Talking to the Embalmer after handing in all the items and before aiding the Carpenter

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Oh, it's you. You've already gathered everything I need. I'm sure you have plenty of other things to be doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I do still need to speak to the carpenter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Oh, it's you. I hear you're helping us now. To be honest, I'm still not thrilled to have you around, but so be it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that's probably the best I can ask for, given the situation. Anyway, the High Priest said you needed help gathering some supplies for the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: That's right. I need three things, but the quarantine is making some of them hard to obtain.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: First, I need some salt. Normally I get mine from the salt lake just north of the city. Just fill a bucket from the lake and then use the suntrap outside to separate the salt from the water.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Next, I need some tree sap. You can use a knife to extract some from a tree, but make sure you have a bucket ready. Most coniferous evergreen trees should have some. They're the cone-shaped ones.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Finally, I need some linen. That one shouldn't be too hard. The local cloth salesman should have some.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Well that was unexpected. Thank you.

sections.title: Aiding the Carpenter
Talking to the Carpenter again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Do you have that wood for me?

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Thank you. Now, just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: There we go. All done.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: I imagine the High Priest will be down in the pyramid by now. You should take that to him as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: I'll be here when you do.

sections.title: Talking to the Carpenter after handing in the logs and before aiding the Embalmer

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. I'm still working on that symbol. Do you have something else you can do in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I need to help out the embalmer.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Well you're probably best off doing that then. I'm sure I'll have it ready by the time you're done.

sections.title: Talking to the Carpenter after handing in the logs and after aiding the Embalmer

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. The symbol is all finished now.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: I imagine the High Priest will be down in the pyramid by now. You should take that to him as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. The High Priest says that you're here to help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. He asked me to come and speak to you about a holy symbol for the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Ah yes, it needs to be made from willow. Thing is, there's a small problem with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this the part where you need me to get you something?

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Wow, you're quick. I'm all out of willow, and due to the quarantine, I'm unable to leave the city to get more. However, the High Priest mentioned that a special exception has been made for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning I can leave the city and get you some. Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's actually no need for me to go anywhere. I already have some willow logs here.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Thank you. Now, just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: There we go. All done.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: I imagine the High Priest will be down in the pyramid by now. You should take that to him as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the Embalmer after aiding both him and the Carpenter

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Embalmer
lines.text: Well I have lots to do, so good day.

sections.title: Talking to the Carpenter after aiding both him and the Embalmer

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Hello. Have you given the symbol to the High Priest yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Well you should as soon as possible. He'll be down in the pyramid by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, there's a slight problem with the symbol.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: What? But I made it to exactly match the drawings from the High Priest. I can't believe this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm sure the symbol itself is fine. The problem is... I lost it.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Agh, you fool! Is stupidity the newest plague in town?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you please calm down? I gave you plenty of willow. I'm sure there's some left over for another symbol.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Fine. Just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: There we go. All done. Try not to lose this one.

lines.speaker: Carpenter
lines.text: Now you should take that to the High Priest as soon as possible. He'll be down in the pyramid by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

sections.title: Talking to Raetul after buying his linen
Reconsecrating the Tomb
Entering Klenter's pyramid to attend the ceremony

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think the High Priest mentioned something about needing a cat to enter the pyramid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey puss! Think you can open this door again?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meeeow.

sections.title: Flashback 3: Jumping over the pit after the ceremony has begun
Attempting to jump over the pit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to hide the mistress's symbol in the ceremonial room...

sections.title: Attempting to hide the symbol under the table

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... It must be somewhere more secret...

sections.title: Hiding the symbol in a sarcophagus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... Now the mistress can enter the pyramid whenever she pleases...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The wanderer! She's going to attack the ceremony! I need to warn them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to hide the mistress's symbol in the ceremonial room...

sections.title: The Devourer revealed
Opening the door to the eastern chamber

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Ah there you are. Do you have the holy symbol?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! We can't begin the ceremony!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What? But we've already started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the wanderer is coming!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Wanderer
lines.text: You have done well, adventurer. All my brother's dogs in one place, with no one around to save them.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It can't be... It's the Devourer!

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: Adventurer, kill these pathetic priests.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not your slave any more!

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: Shame. Not to worry though, I have plenty more.

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: You! Priest of the dog lord. You are now mine. Kill your brethren!

lines.speaker: Possessed Priest
lines.text: Yes mistress...

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: Have fun...

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Protect us, adventurer! We must complete the ceremony!

sections.title: Defeating the posessed priest
Talking to the Carpenter after saving the ceremony
Talking to a priest in the temple after saving the ceremony

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: You have surprised me... I commend you for your efforts.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest after saving the ceremony

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Thank you so much, adventurer. Thanks to you, we were able to complete the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it was the least I could do. After all, it was sort of my fault all this happened.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well on that note, what exactly did happen there? That was the Devourer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems the wanderer that hypnotised me was actually the Devourer all along.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing the reason she had me steal that burial jar was because she knew you'd have to gather here to reconsecrate the tomb again.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: She wanted to kill us all in one go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and she had me plant an unholy symbol here to allow her to teleport right in.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well once again, thank you. Without you, the Devourer would have struck a devastating blow against Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's that holy symbol, by the way.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Thank you. We wouldn't have been able to complete the ceremony without that in our presence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We have a few things to finish here, but then we'll head back up into the city. Please join us there.

sections.title: Flashback 4: Leaving the ceremonial room

lines.speaker: Icthlarin
lines.text: And what do we have here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must do as the mistress commands...

lines.speaker: Icthlarin
lines.text: Did my sister really think that I would not sense her presence here?

lines.speaker: Icthlarin
lines.text: Amascut! Release your hold on this human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I guess that explains why Amascut's control over me was broken. Not every day the god of the dead comes to your rescue.

sections.title: Finishing Up
Speaking with a priest at the temple
Speaking with the High Priest at the temple

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Ah, there you are. You were gone for quite a while. As a thank you for what you've done, I have a little trinket for you. I'm sure you'll get a huge amount of enjoyment out of it.

sections.title: Trying to enter the pyramid after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it would be best if I stayed out of this tomb after what happened last time.
